topic: How do you onboard a new PRO officer without losing track of open cases mid-handover?
format: Walk through the exact day-in-the-life of a Sanad office manager handling 20+ client companies with no case tracking system — every painful step in sequence
post:
Your PRO officer resigned on Thursday. New person starts Sunday.

Somewhere in his phone are 6 WhatsApp threads with client companies. Somewhere in his Excel is a tab called "April renewals — FINAL v3." Somewhere in his head is the context for 40 open cases across 20 clients.

You have 48 hours to hand that over.

This is the real cost of running your Sanad office on personal tools. Not the daily inefficiency — you've made peace with that. It's the moment someone walks out, and the entire operation walks out with them.

Cases go quiet. Clients call asking for updates you don't have. A work permit renewal due in 5 days gets missed because nobody knew it existed.

That's not a documentation problem. It's a systems problem.

When every case sits in a shared platform — assigned to an officer, timestamped, documents attached, expiry dates visible — a new PRO officer can open a laptop Sunday morning and actually know where things stand. No digging through chat histories. No guessing what "FINAL v3" means.

924 licensed Sanad offices in Oman. Most of them one resignation away from a very bad week.

Does your office survive a handover right now — or does the knowledge leave with the person?

Try the Sanad AI Assistant free — instant answers on work permits, visas & government fees: https://www.thesmartpro.io/sanad/assistant?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=sanad-pro
